You’ll also get a feel for what other parents commonly experience, without any pressure or overload. Think of this as a calm, parent-friendly roadmap to help you move forward with confidence, one simple step at a time. When Can a Kid Transition to a Blanket? 				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
					<p>Does your back scream “OUCH!” every morning like an angry alarm clock? You’re not alone! Millions of people start their day with aching backs, and the problem might be hiding right under your nose – or rather, under your entire body. Your mattress plays a huge role in how your back feels each morning. When you sleep in weird positions or on the wrong type of bed, your spine gets twisted, and your muscles work overtime all night long. Fun fact: We spend about one-third of our lives sleeping, which means you’ll spend roughly 25 years of your life on your mattress! The good news is that finding the right mattress can turn your painful mornings into pain-free ones. Your back deserves a good night’s sleep, and we’re here to help you find it! Understanding Back Pain and Sleep Your back can hurt when you wake up for several reasons.				</div>

				<div class="elementor-element elementor-element-f514fb9 excerpt2 elementor-widget elementor-widget-theme-post-excerpt" data-id="f514fb9" data-element_type="widget" data-e-type="widget" data-widget_type="theme-post-excerpt.default">
				<div class="elementor-widget-container">
					<p>Here’s a little secret most of us don’t want to think about: your pillow might be older than your phone. And unlike your phone, it’s been collecting sweat, skin cells, dust mites, and maybe even mold for years. Sounds cozy? Not so much. That’s why knowing how often you should replace pillows is so important. A good pillow keeps your neck supported, your sleep comfortable, and your allergies at bay. But once it loses its shape or collects too much gunk, it can actually hurt your sleep and your health. In this guide, we’ll talk about the best time to switch your pillows, the signs they need to go, and some easy tips to make them last longer. Let’s get into it, your neck will be grateful.
